summary: Closed, apparently unmerged PR that claimed to decompile a batch of pointer-math-heavy functions across gmresultplayer, gmregclear, grmaterial, grcastle, grrcruise, itkamex, and itzgshell. The PR body explicitly grouped these because the matches used M2C_FIELD, raw pointer arithmetic, and u8* casts that might need discussion on match quality versus code style. It also added substantial Claude/overnight decomp automation documentation and scripts. The test plan claimed successful ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff matches, but there were no issue comments, reviews, or review comments in the slice, and the PR was closed shortly after creation without merge evidence.

key_files.role: Implemented fn_80179F84 and fn_8017A9B4, added tobj include, externs for result globals, and a bitfield/padded type for lbl_8046E3AC.
Implemented fn_8017F14C and refined lbl_80472D28_t padding to expose x10A.
Implemented grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750 using Ground user_data, byte-offset access, and PAD_STACK.
Implemented grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698; changed grMaterial_801C9470 from HSD_JObj wrapper shape to Item_GObj/CommandInfo forwarding.
Implemented fn_802010A4 with table traversal via gp->gv.pad_0 plus byte-offset entry fields.
Implemented it_802CA49C, it_802CA6A0, and itKamex_UnkMotion1_Phys using item/article data and M2C_FIELD offsets.
Implemented several zgshell motion/collision/helper functions and added itGShell_HurtInit local struct.
New helper script for overnight decomp workflow: stream JSON parsing, result parsing, token usage, stub filtering, context trimming, asm extraction, cutoff/progress helpers.
Large new automation script for batch/overnight decomp attempts.
New repository guide for Claude Code covering build commands, style, naming, architecture, workflow, matching tips, and permuter usage.

what_changed: Replaced multiple /// # stubs with C implementations in game-mode, stage, and item modules; the PR body named functions including fn_80179F84, fn_8017A9B4, fn_802010A4, it_802CA49C, it_802CA6A0, itKamex_UnkMotion1_Phys, fn_8017F14C, grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9698, grMaterial_801C9604, grCastle_801CD960, and grCastle_801CF750.

Updated headers for newly typed function signatures, replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete return/argument types such as void fn_80179F84(HSD_JObj*), void fn_8017A9B4(int), s32 fn_8017F14C(void*), and void fn_802010A4(Ground_GObj*, s32, u8*).

Added or refined local types/externs where needed: gmregclear exposed lbl_80472D28_t.x10A by splitting padding; gmresultplayer added externs for lbl_8046E1B0, lbl_8046E38C, lbl_8046E39C, and lbl_8046E3AC_t; itzgshell added itGShell_HurtInit.
Used explicit pointer arithmetic and field-offset access in several matches: casts like (u8*) gp + 0xE0, (u8*) gp + 0xD0, gp->gv.pad_0 + 0x6C, and M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xE3C/0xE40).
Added PAD_STACK in functions where the generated C needed stack-frame adjustment, including grCastle_801CF750, it_802CA49C, and it_802DE0F0.
Added substantial automation/tooling files for Claude-assisted decomp batches: decomp_helpers.py, decomp_overnight.sh, schedule_overnight.sh, find_stubs.py, find_all_stubs.sh, and CLAUDE.md.
The PR body claimed ninja builds, ninja diff no regressions, and 100% objdiff matches for all targeted functions.
smart_moves: The author explicitly called out the risk area: these matches used M2C_FIELD, raw pointer arithmetic, and u8* casts, so the PR framed them as needing possible discussion on match quality versus source style rather than presenting them as unquestioned final-quality decomp.
Header prototypes were updated alongside C implementations, reducing future ambiguity around call signatures and making the newly decompiled functions more searchable.
Splitting gmregclear struct padding to add x10A is a reusable tactic when one known field can be recovered without fully solving the surrounding global layout.

The grMaterial_801C9470 change preserved the wrapper behavior while adapting its signature to pass Item_GObj* and CommandInfo* through to grMaterial_801C9490, suggesting attention to call-site semantics rather than only the callee body.

The new CLAUDE.md captured project-wide style and matching guidance, including avoiding raw pointer arithmetic in ideal decomp code, preferring true matches, using PAD_STACK for frame-size fixes, and proper struct/padding practices.

decomp_helpers.py moved overnight automation logic into testable subcommands and parsed Claude stream JSON result events structurally rather than relying only on grep-style conversational text matching.
decomp_lessons: Pointer-math matches can be useful to remove stubs, but this PR is evidence that they should be treated as a separate review class: matching assembly alone may not mean the C is acceptable long-term style.

When an accessed field is hidden inside padding, a minimal struct refinement can improve clarity without requiring a complete struct recovery; gmregclear’s x10A field is the clearest example in this slice.

Ground and item code often exposes decomp pressure around partially known data: gobj->user_data, gp->gv.pad_0, and item variable unions may need temporary byte-offset access until better structs exist.
PAD_STACK should be documented at the function where it is required and kept narrow; this PR used it in a few specific decompiled functions rather than globally changing stack behavior.
If a function uses raw offsets such as 0xE3C, 0xE40, 0xE2C, 0xC0, 0xD0, or 0x40, a future cleanup query should try to recover the owning struct fields and replace M2C_FIELD/casts with named members.
Large automation/documentation additions mixed into a decomp PR can make review harder; future work may want separate PRs for tooling/docs versus actual function decompilation.
naming_conventions: The added CLAUDE.md states that functions should be prefixed with their file/module name, with examples like ftCo_Attack100_Enter, lbVector_sin, and ftFox_LaserOnDeath.
The added CLAUDE.md states file names use lowerCamelCase while function names use UpperCamelCase, with standard library reimplementations using lowercase.

The added CLAUDE.md lists module prefixes such as ft for fighter, it for item, gr for ground/stage, gm for game, lb for library, cm for camera, ef for effect, if for interface, mn for menu, mp for map/collision, pl for player, sc for scene, ty for trophy, and vi for visual.
The added CLAUDE.md recommends struct members be labeled with hex offsets and unknown/padding names such as x10_thing and pad_xx.
Existing placeholder naming remained for many decompiled functions, such as fn_80179F84, fn_8017A9B4, fn_8017F14C, and fn_802010A4, indicating behavior was not yet confidently named.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use M2C_FIELD for hard-to-type recovered offsets when exact matching is possible but owning struct layout is not yet established.
Use u8* casts for byte-granular offset math when accessing partially understood structures or packed tables, as seen in grCastle, grMaterial, grRCruise, gmresultplayer, and gmregclear.
Use PAD_STACK(n) when the C body matches semantically but the stack frame is off by a known byte count; examples in the diff include PAD_STACK(16) and PAD_STACK(8).
For table scans, model loop stride explicitly: fn_802010A4 walks 17 entries with entry size 0x18 and checks a s16 id at entry + 2.
For small wrapper functions, preserve forwarding shape and update signatures to match the actual command callback type, as in grMaterial_801C9470 forwarding to grMaterial_801C9490.

The added automation script guidance mentions ignoring pure register swaps until permuter work, using ABS/MIN/MAX for common patterns, and preferring structured for/while control flow over goto/labels, but those are from the added CLAUDE.md rather than reviewer feedback.

review_feedback.feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in this slice.

The only explicit quality note is from the PR author: these pointer-math functions were grouped separately because M2C_FIELD/raw pointer arithmetic/u8* casts may need discussion on match quality versus code style.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replaced M2C_FIELD offsets 0xE3C and 0xE40 in itkamex with named Item or Pokemon fields.
Check whether fn_80179F84, fn_8017A9B4, fn_8017F14C, and fn_802010A4 were later merged, renamed, or reworked with better structs.
Search for later struct recovery around Ground offsets 0x40, 0xC0, 0xD0, and gp->gv.pad_0 + 0x6C used by grmaterial, grcastle, and grrcruise.
Determine whether the Claude/overnight scripts and CLAUDE.md were intentionally accepted elsewhere or dropped because this PR was closed unmerged.
Compare this PR against any successor branch/PR from johnwinston to see if the pointer-math decomp batch was split into cleaner reviewable pieces.
